pakhom 1.0.0

    • Methodology Modes: Choosing Mode 1, 2, or 3

Stamp a JSON file with a methodology envelope

Source: R/output_stamping.R
stamp_methodology_json.Rd

JSON files cannot accept comment-style headers (the format has no comment syntax), so the stamp is added as a top-level _methodology_stamp key on an envelope object that wraps the original payload as _payload. Idempotent: re-stamping a file that already has a _methodology_stamp envelope no-ops.

Usage

stamp_methodology_json(json_path, mode, run_id = NULL)

Arguments

json_path

Path to a JSON file (will be re-written with the stamp).

mode

Character methodology mode.

run_id

Optional character run identifier.

Value

Invisibly returns json_path.

Details

Output shape:


{
  "_methodology_stamp": {
    "mode": "reflexive_scaffold",
    "label": "M1 - Reflexive Scaffold",
    "run_id": "run_2026-...",
    "stamped_at": "2026-..."
  },
  "_payload": <original JSON object>
}

Consumers reading the original payload should look at json[["_payload"]] when the envelope is present, falling back to the document root otherwise. Per AC4 every output gets a stamp; per AC1 the consumer's parser is the one place the envelope is acknowledged.

On this page
